title: Skills for Software Engineer Resume
metaDescription: Technical and soft skills to list on a software engineer resume. Skill clusters, bullet examples, and keywords that get past ATS and recruiters.
intro: A strong software engineer resume balances technical depth with clear evidence of impact and collaboration. This guide groups skills into clusters and shows how to turn them into compelling resume bullets that pass ATS and resonate with hiring managers.
bulletExamples: Designed and shipped a new API used by 5 internal services; reduced p99 latency by 30% and improved error handling.
Led migration of legacy monolith to microservices; cut deployment time from 2 hours to 15 minutes and improved reliability.
Mentored 3 junior engineers; established code review guidelines and on-call runbooks adopted across the team.
body:
List skills in a dedicated section and reinforce them in your experience bullets. Use the same terms as the job description where they genuinely apply—ATS and recruiters look for keyword match.

Prefer impact-focused bullets (what you built, for whom, and what improved) over task lists. Tailoring your resume to each role will surface the right mix of languages, systems, and soft skills. WadeCV can help you align your existing experience with software engineer job descriptions and highlight your strongest technical and collaborative wins.

faq.question: How many skills should I list on a software engineer resume?
faq.answer: Focus on 10–15 relevant technical skills plus a few soft skills. Match the job description and reinforce them in your experience bullets rather than listing every technology you've touched.

commonMistakes: Listing outdated or irrelevant technologies
Generic soft skills without evidence in bullets
Keyword stuffing; keep language natural

title: Skills for Product Manager Resume
metaDescription: Skills to put on a product manager resume: discovery, prioritization, data, and collaboration. Bullet examples and keywords for PM roles.
intro: Product managers need to show they can drive strategy, work with data, and ship outcomes with engineering and design. This guide outlines skill clusters and resume bullet examples that resonate with PM hiring managers and ATS.
bulletExamples: Owned roadmap for core onboarding flow; increased activation by 25% through iterative A/B tests and user research.
Led discovery and prioritization for 3 product areas; aligned engineering, design, and leadership on quarterly OKRs.
Drove go-to-market for new feature with 50K+ users in first quarter; presented results to C-suite and board.
body:
PM resumes should emphasise outcomes: adoption, revenue, retention, or efficiency gains. Use metrics in every bullet where possible.

Balance strategic work (roadmap, discovery) with execution (shipping, stakeholder management). Tailoring your resume to the company's stage and product type will improve fit. WadeCV can help you reframe your PM experience to match job descriptions and surface your strongest impact.

title: Skills for Customer Service Resume
metaDescription: Customer service resume skills: communication, problem-solving, tools, and soft skills. Bullet examples and keywords for CS and support roles.
intro: Customer service and support roles value communication, empathy, and efficiency. This guide groups skills into clusters and shows how to write resume bullets that demonstrate impact and readiness for higher-tier or leadership roles.
bulletExamples: Handled 50+ customer contacts daily with top quartile CSAT and resolution rate; identified recurring issues and proposed FAQ updates that cut repeat contacts by 20%.
Trained 5 new hires on product and systems; created troubleshooting playbook adopted by entire support team.
Reduced average handle time by 15% while maintaining quality scores; led pilot for new chat tool rollout.
body: Lead with metrics: volume handled, CSAT or NPS, resolution rates, and time saved. Show progression—taking on harder cases, training others, or improving processes. Use keywords from the job description (tools, channels, and soft skills) so ATS and recruiters see a match. Tailoring your resume to each company's support model will help. WadeCV can help you align your customer service experience with job descriptions and highlight your strongest contributions.

Tailoring your resume to the industry (e.g. product, marketing, finance, healthcare) will strengthen your application. WadeCV can help you align your analyst experience with data analyst job descriptions and surface your best outcomes.

title: Skills for Project Manager Resume

intro: Project managers need a blend of hard skills (planning tools, methodologies) and soft skills (leadership, communication, conflict resolution). This guide organises PM skills into clusters and shows how to present them on a resume that passes ATS and resonates with hiring managers.
bulletExamples: Delivered $2.4M ERP implementation 2 weeks ahead of schedule by restructuring sprint planning and eliminating 3 recurring blocker categories.

body: List methodology certifications (PMP, PMI-ACP, PRINCE2) prominently — they're often filtered on by ATS. In your experience section, show delivery outcomes: on-time %, budget variance, and stakeholder satisfaction. WadeCV can help you surface the most relevant PM skills and outcomes for each specific role.

faq.question: Which project management skills are most in demand in 2026?
faq.answer: Agile fluency (Scrum, Kanban), digital project tools (Jira, Asana, Monday.com), and stakeholder communication skills top most job descriptions. PMP certification remains a strong differentiator for mid-to-senior roles.

commonMistakes: Listing tools without showing scale or complexity of projects managed
No delivery metrics — on-time rate, budget variance
Omitting certification acronyms that ATS scans for

title: Skills for Business Analyst Resume
metaDescription: Business analyst skills to list on your resume. Key competencies, ATS keywords, and impact-driven bullet examples for BA roles across IT, finance, and operations.
intro: Business analysts need analytical rigour, communication clarity, and the ability to bridge business and technology. This guide covers the skill clusters, tools, and bullet examples that make a BA resume stand out in ATS screening and hiring manager review.
bulletExamples: Gathered and documented requirements for a $1.2M CRM migration; requirements accepted with zero rework cycles, project delivered on schedule.
Built a Power BI dashboard tracking 12 operational KPIs, reducing weekly reporting time from 6 hours to 45 minutes.
Facilitated 8 cross-functional workshops to map as-is processes, identifying $300K in annual savings through automation.
body: BA resumes are won or lost on specificity. Name the domain (healthcare, financial services, retail), the tools (SQL, Power BI, Visio), and the outcome (savings, time saved, rework eliminated). CBAP or CCBA certification adds credibility for senior roles. WadeCV can align your BA skills with each job's domain and toolset.

faq.question: Do I need SQL to get a business analyst job?
faq.answer: For most modern BA roles, yes — especially in tech, finance, and data-driven organisations. Even basic SQL (SELECT, JOINs, aggregations) to pull data for analysis is increasingly expected. It's worth learning even if not required by every posting.

commonMistakes: Generic bullets about 'gathering requirements' without specifics
No mention of domain expertise (finance, healthcare, IT)
Missing tool keywords: SQL, Power BI, Visio

title: Skills for Operations Manager Resume
metaDescription: Operations manager skills to list on your resume. Competency clusters, ATS keywords, and impact-driven bullet examples for ops manager roles across industries.
intro: Operations managers need a combination of process improvement expertise, people leadership, and financial acumen. This guide covers the skill clusters that appear most often in ops manager job descriptions, with bullet examples that show measurable impact.
bulletExamples: Led Lean initiative across 3 production lines, reducing cycle time by 22% and saving $480K annually in labour and waste.
Managed $4.2M operations budget across 2 sites, ending each of 3 consecutive years under budget.
Built and scaled a 45-person operations team over 18 months, reducing voluntary attrition from 28% to 14%.
body: Operations manager resumes are judged on measurable improvement. Every major bullet should show a before/after: cost reduced, time saved, quality improved, team scaled. Lean or Six Sigma certification (Green Belt, Black Belt) is a differentiator. WadeCV can tailor your ops skills to the specific industry and scope each employer is hiring for.

faq.question: Is Six Sigma certification required for an operations manager job?
faq.answer: Not required, but it's a strong differentiator — especially in manufacturing, logistics, and healthcare operations. Even a Green Belt shows commitment to process improvement methodology. Many employers list it as 'preferred' rather than required.

commonMistakes: Describing scope without outcomes — 'managed daily operations' is not enough
No financial metrics (budget owned, cost saved)
Omitting Lean/Six Sigma training even if informal

title: Skills for Cybersecurity Resume
metaDescription: Cybersecurity skills to list on your resume in 2026. Technical skill clusters, certifications, and ATS keywords for SOC analyst, security engineer, and infosec roles.
intro: Cybersecurity roles demand deep technical breadth — tools, platforms, protocols — plus analytical skills and the ability to communicate risk to non-technical stakeholders. This guide covers the skill clusters, certifications, and bullet examples that make a cybersecurity resume stand out.
bulletExamples: Triaged 150+ security alerts daily in Splunk SIEM; reduced mean time to detect (MTTD) from 4.2 hours to 38 minutes through tuned correlation rules.
Conducted penetration testing across 12 web applications; identified 3 critical vulnerabilities (OWASP Top 10) and coordinated remediation with dev teams.
Led ISO 27001 gap assessment and remediation plan; organisation achieved certification within 9 months.
body: Cybersecurity resumes need both certifications and real-world experience. Certs without context (no incidents investigated, no tools deployed) look weak. Show scale: alerts/day, coverage area, compliance scope. Government/cleared roles should note clearance level. WadeCV can align your security skills to each role's specific focus — SOC analyst, AppSec, GRC, or cloud security.

faq.question: What cybersecurity certification should I get first?
faq.answer: CompTIA Security+ is the most widely recognised entry-level certification and a prerequisite for many DoD contractor roles. For hands-on penetration testing, OSCP is the gold standard. CISSP is for experienced practitioners targeting senior or management roles.

commonMistakes: Listing certifications without linking them to real work experience
No metrics: alerts handled, MTTD/MTTR, coverage area
Missing key tool names that ATS scans for

title: Skills for Digital Marketing Resume
metaDescription: Digital marketing skills to list on your resume in 2026. Channel-by-channel skill clusters, ATS keywords, and results-driven bullet examples for marketing roles.
intro: Digital marketers must show fluency across multiple channels — SEO, paid search, email, social, and analytics. Employers scan for specific tools and platforms, not just generic 'marketing' skills. This guide organises digital marketing skills by channel and shows how to quantify your impact.
bulletExamples: Grew organic search traffic 140% YoY through a technical SEO overhaul and 60-piece content calendar — driven by keyword gap analysis in Ahrefs.
Managed $350K annual Google Ads budget; reduced CPA by 31% through audience segmentation, negative keyword expansion, and landing page A/B testing.
Built and optimised email welcome sequence in Klaviyo; sequence now drives 18% of total monthly revenue with 42% open rate.
body: Digital marketing resumes are strongest when they show channel-specific results with real numbers. Don't just say you 'managed social media' — specify the platform, audience size, and engagement or growth achieved. Tool fluency is essential; list specific platforms for each channel. WadeCV can tailor your marketing skills to each employer's channel mix and industry.

faq.question: What are the most in-demand digital marketing skills in 2026?
faq.answer: Paid media (especially Google Ads and Meta Ads), marketing analytics (GA4, Looker Studio, attribution), and SEO remain top priorities. AI fluency — using tools like ChatGPT or Jasper in content workflows — is increasingly mentioned in job descriptions.

commonMistakes: Generic 'social media' without platform, audience size, or metrics
No mention of specific tools — ATS filters on platform names
Missing ROAS, CPA, or growth metrics on paid campaigns

title: Skills for Machine Learning Resume
metaDescription: Machine learning skills to list on your resume. Technical skill clusters, framework keywords, and impact-driven bullet examples for ML engineer and data science roles.
intro: Machine learning roles demand a specific combination of maths fundamentals, Python/framework depth, and engineering skills to take models to production. This guide covers the skill clusters, keywords, and bullet examples that help ML resumes get noticed.
bulletExamples: Trained and deployed a BERT-based document classifier achieving 94% accuracy; model now processes 2M+ documents/day in production on SageMaker.
Reduced model retraining pipeline from 6 hours to 45 minutes by migrating to distributed training on AWS, saving $18K/month in compute.
Built a recommendation system for 5M users using collaborative filtering; A/B test showed 12% lift in session engagement.
body: ML resumes live at the intersection of research depth and engineering credibility. Show both: models you designed and deployed, with scale (users served, requests/second) and business impact (revenue, engagement, cost). Open-source contributions and Kaggle rankings are worth including. WadeCV can tailor your ML skills to each role's focus — NLP, computer vision, MLOps, or LLM engineering.

faq.question: Should I list both PyTorch and TensorFlow on my ML resume?
faq.answer: Yes, if you have genuine experience with both. PyTorch dominates research and most modern ML teams; TensorFlow/Keras is more common in enterprise settings. List the one you're strongest in first and note your proficiency level if they differ significantly.

commonMistakes: Only showing Jupyter notebook projects — no production deployments
Listing frameworks without showing what you built or the scale of impact
No mention of model monitoring, drift detection, or retraining pipelines

title: Skills for Cloud Computing Resume
metaDescription: Cloud computing skills to list on your resume for AWS, Azure, and GCP roles. Technical clusters, certifications, and results-focused bullet examples for cloud engineers.
intro: Cloud computing roles require a mix of architecture knowledge, hands-on infrastructure skills, and security awareness. Whether you're targeting AWS, Azure, or GCP, employers want to see specific services, certifications, and real deployments — not just general cloud familiarity.

skillClusters.name: Cloud platforms
items: AWS (EC2, S3, RDS, Lambda, EKS, CloudFront)
Azure (AKS, App Service, Azure SQL, Entra ID)
GCP (GKE, BigQuery, Cloud Run, Vertex AI)

bulletExamples: Designed and deployed multi-region AWS infrastructure for a SaaS platform serving 50K daily active users; achieved 99.97% uptime over 12 months.
Migrated 40+ microservices from on-premises to EKS using Terraform; reduced infrastructure cost by $120K/year and deployment time from 4 hours to 12 minutes.
Implemented AWS WAF and GuardDuty; blocked 98% of automated attack traffic and achieved SOC 2 Type II compliance in 6 months.
body: Cloud resumes must show specific services (not just 'AWS') with real outcomes: reliability metrics, cost savings, and migration scope. Certifications matter — list them with year achieved. Show IaC ownership (you wrote the Terraform, not just used it). WadeCV can tailor your cloud skills to the specific provider and architecture style each employer uses.

faq.question: Is one cloud certification enough to get a cloud engineer job?
faq.answer: AWS SAA or Azure AZ-104 is a solid foundation, but employers increasingly want to see real project experience alongside certifications. A GitHub portfolio with Terraform modules or Kubernetes deployments carries more weight than certifications alone.

commonMistakes: Listing 'AWS' without specifying which services you've worked with in production
No reliability or cost metrics for infrastructure you built
Missing IaC tools — employers expect Terraform or CloudFormation fluency

title: Skills for Python Developer Resume
metaDescription: Python skills to list on your resume in 2026. Framework clusters, ATS keywords, and impact-driven bullet examples for backend, data, and automation Python roles.
intro: Python is the most in-demand programming language across web development, data engineering, ML, and DevOps automation. Employers want to see specific frameworks and libraries alongside real projects. This guide covers the Python skill clusters that appear most often in job descriptions.
bulletExamples: Built a FastAPI microservice processing 10K requests/second with sub-20ms p99 latency; deployed on Kubernetes with auto-scaling and blue/green deployments.
Wrote automated ETL pipeline in Python/Airflow ingesting 50GB/day from 12 sources into PostgreSQL; reduced analyst data prep time from 3 hours to 5 minutes.
Refactored legacy Django monolith into async FastAPI services; improved throughput 4x and reduced server costs by $40K/year.
body: Python developer resumes must show the domain (web, data, ML, DevOps) alongside framework fluency. Don't just list libraries — show what you built with them and at what scale. Test coverage and code quality habits (pytest, type hints, CI) signal senior-level engineering. WadeCV can tailor your Python skills to each role's specific stack and domain focus.

faq.question: Should I list all Python frameworks I know on my resume?
faq.answer: List the frameworks relevant to the role you're applying for. A web role wants FastAPI/Django/Flask; a data role wants Pandas/NumPy/Spark; an ML role wants PyTorch/scikit-learn. Tailoring your skills section to each job description is more effective than exhaustive lists.

commonMistakes: Listing 'Python' without any frameworks or domain context
No scale or performance metrics on projects
Omitting testing, typing, or code quality signals
